elements.name: Model Discovery and Identification
elements.description: Can a user or system discover and uniquely identify this model?

sub_elements.name: Persistent Identifier
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.path: 'https://huggingface.co/SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M'; model_details.references[0]: same URL; base_model: 'westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'
sub_elements.quality_note: HuggingFace Hub identifier provided as both path and reference. The ID is unique and resolvable.

sub_elements.name: Model Name and Description Completeness
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.name: 'SaProtHub SubCell Localization 650M (LoRA-fine-tuned SaProt)'; short_description: 93 chars; overview: 437 chars with architecture details and performance
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive name, short description, and detailed overview explaining model purpose, architecture, and performance metrics.

sub_elements.name: Tags / Pipeline Tag for Searchability
sub_elements.evidence: tags: 8 tags including 'protein-language-model', 'subcellular-localization', 'bioinformatics', 'protein-classification', 'saprot', 'lora', 'deeploc', 'structure-aware'; pipeline_tag: 'text-classification'; model_category: 'protein-language-model'
sub_elements.quality_note: Excellent tag coverage (8 relevant domain-specific tags) with appropriate pipeline_tag and model_category.

sub_elements.name: Model Landing Page or Repository URL
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references: 5 URLs including HF Hub model page, base model page, GitHub repo, UniProt documentation, and DeepLoc service
sub_elements.quality_note: Multiple accessible landing pages provided, including primary HF Hub location, base model, code repository, and domain-specific documentation.

sub_elements.name: Library / Framework Identification
sub_elements.evidence: framework: 'PyTorch'; framework_version: 'torch>=2.0'; library_name: 'transformers'
sub_elements.quality_note: Framework, version constraint, and library explicitly specified.

elements.name: Model Access and Distribution
elements.description: Can the model weights, code, and inference pipeline be located and used?

sub_elements.name: Weight Distribution Mechanism Defined
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.path: 'https://huggingface.co/SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M'
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear HuggingFace Hub path for weight distribution provided.

sub_elements.name: Code Repository Available
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[2]: 'https://github.com/westlake-repl/SaProt'
sub_elements.quality_note: GitHub repository link provided for the SaProt codebase.

sub_elements.name: Inference API / Usage Example Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.documentation contains usage example: 'from saprot_hub import load_model / model = load_model("SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M") / pred = model.predict(sa_sequence)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Runnable Python code snippet provided with clear API usage pattern.

sub_elements.name: Input / Output Specification
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.input_format: 'Structure-aware (SA) protein sequence — amino-acid sequence interleaved with AlphaFold2 Foldseek structural tokens'; input_format_map with type, encoding, max_length; output_format: 'Classification label (integer 0-9)'; output_format_map with shape '[B, 10]' and interpretation of 10 classes
sub_elements.quality_note: Detailed input/output specifications including format, encoding, shape, and class interpretation.

sub_elements.name: Model File Format Specified
sub_elements.evidence: No explicit mention of file format (safetensors, ONNX, PyTorch state_dict, etc.) in model_details.path or model_parameters
sub_elements.quality_note: While HuggingFace Hub path is provided, the specific serialization format (safetensors vs PyTorch .bin vs ONNX) is not explicitly stated.

elements.name: Model Reuse and Interoperability
elements.description: Is sufficient information provided to reuse and integrate this model with others?

sub_elements.name: License Terms Allow Reuse
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.licenses[0].identifier: 'MIT'; license_name: 'MIT License'; license_link: 'https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT'
sub_elements.quality_note: SPDX-style MIT license explicitly stated with link, allowing broad reuse.

sub_elements.name: Standard Framework / Format Used
sub_elements.evidence: framework: 'PyTorch'; framework_version: 'torch>=2.0'; library_name: 'transformers'
sub_elements.quality_note: Standard framework (PyTorch) with version constraint and HuggingFace transformers library specified.

sub_elements.name: Base Model or Foundation Lineage Stated
sub_elements.evidence: base_model: 'westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'; model_details.references[1]: 'https://huggingface.co/westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'; overview states 'LoRA fine-tune of westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'
sub_elements.quality_note: Base model clearly identified with HuggingFace Hub ID, reference link, and explicit fine-tuning relationship.

sub_elements.name: Supported Tasks Declared
sub_elements.evidence: pipeline_tag: 'text-classification'; considerations.use_cases: 4 use cases including 'Predicting subcellular localization for protein-annotation pipelines', 'Filtering proteomics candidates by compartment', 'Benchmarking against DeepLoc 2.0', 'Educational reference for LoRA fine-tuning'
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear pipeline task classification with multiple concrete use cases provided.

sub_elements.name: Reproducibility Artifacts Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters includes optimizer (AdamW), learning_rate (0.0005), batch_size (64), training_epochs (100), loss_function, optimization_techniques with LoRA parameters, mixed precision; GitHub repo link provided
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive hyperparameters provided with optimizer details, LoRA configuration, and code repository link sufficient for reproduction.

elements.name: Ethical Use and Responsible AI
elements.description: Does the model card provide clear information about risks, bias, and ethical oversight?

sub_elements.name: Ethical Considerations Documented
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations[0].name: 'Mis-localization in drug discovery pipelines'; mitigation_strategy: 'Treat predictions as a screening hypothesis, not ground truth. Confirm with experimental localization...'
sub_elements.quality_note: Named ethical risk with concrete mitigation strategy addressing downstream pharmaceutical use.

sub_elements.name: Known Model / Output Bias Disclosed
sub_elements.evidence: bias_model: 'Predictions reflect the species and protein-family distribution of the DeepLoc training set (SwissProt-biased toward extensively-studied model organisms)...'; bias_output: 'Class prior matches the DeepLoc training distribution — rare compartments (peroxisome, plastid) are likely under-predicted...'
sub_elements.quality_note: Specific biases disclosed for both model (species bias toward model organisms) and output (class imbalance in predictions).

sub_elements.name: Out-of-Scope and Discouraged Uses
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.out_of_scope_uses: 3 items including 'Diagnosing localization-related diseases without experimental confirmation', 'Predicting localization in species or kingdoms underrepresented in DeepLoc', 'Multi-label localization prediction'
sub_elements.quality_note: Explicit out-of-scope uses documented with concrete examples of discouraged applications.

sub_elements.name: Sensitive Data Use Disclosed
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data[0].sensitive.sensitive_data: ['No human PII; SwissProt protein sequences only']
sub_elements.quality_note: Explicit disclosure that no human PII is present, only SwissProt protein sequences.

sub_elements.name: Intended Users and Stakeholder Impact Statement
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.users[0]: 'Structural biologists and bioinformaticians who want quick subcellular localization predictions...'; tradeoffs[0]: '650M-parameter base provides higher accuracy than the 35M variant (85.75% vs ~80%) at higher inference cost. LoRA keeps trainable parameter count small...'
sub_elements.quality_note: Named user groups (structural biologists, bioinformaticians) and explicit accuracy vs. computational cost tradeoff documented.

elements.name: Model Architecture and Training Composition
elements.description: Can the model's architecture and training composition be understood from metadata?

sub_elements.name: Architecture Described in Detail
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.model_architecture: '650M-parameter SaProt_650M_AF2 base (ESM-style encoder augmented with structure-aware vocabulary from AlphaFold2 Foldseek tokens). LoRA (rank 16, alpha 32, dropout 0) targeting attention query/key/value projections and the feedforward dense layers...'
sub_elements.quality_note: Detailed architecture description including parameter count (650M), base architecture (ESM-style encoder), LoRA configuration (rank 16, alpha 32, dropout 0), and target layers (Q/K/V projections, FFN).

sub_elements.name: Training Data Documented
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data[0]: name 'DeepLoc subcellular localization dataset (SaProtHub mirror)'; link: 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'; description: 'DeepLoc 2.0 benchmark: SwissProt proteins with experimentally-annotated subcellular localizations spanning 10 classes...'
sub_elements.quality_note: Concrete dataset documented with name, link, and detailed description including data source (SwissProt), annotation type (experimentally-validated), and class coverage (10 classes).

sub_elements.name: Hyperparameters Reported
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters: optimizer: 'AdamW', learning_rate: 0.0005, batch_size: 64, training_epochs: 100, loss_function: 'Cross-entropy', optimization_techniques including LoRA parameters, mixed precision FP16, AdamW betas=(0.9, 0.98), weight_decay=0.01
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive hyperparameters including optimizer (AdamW with betas), learning rate, batch size, epochs, loss function, and regularization (weight decay, LoRA dropout).

sub_elements.name: Compute Infrastructure Reported (Extended)
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.description mentions 'Mixed precision (FP16) on a single GPU' but no model_parameters.compute_infrastructure field with hardware type, GPU model, compute hours, or energy use
sub_elements.quality_note: Only minimal infrastructure information provided (single GPU, FP16). Missing specific GPU model, compute hours, and energy consumption.

sub_elements.name: Training / Evaluation Split Defined
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data[0].description: 'Train/validation/test splits provided by the SaProtHub mirror'; quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[0].slice: 'DeepLoc subcellular localization test set (10-way)'
sub_elements.quality_note: Train/validation/test splits explicitly mentioned, and test set performance reported on the designated test split.

elements.name: Model Provenance and Versioning
elements.description: Can a user determine model versions, update history, and provenance?

sub_elements.name: Version Number Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.name: '1.0'
sub_elements.quality_note: Semantic version number provided (1.0).

sub_elements.name: Version Date Documented
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.date: '2024-08-01'
sub_elements.quality_note: ISO 8601 release date provided (2024-08-01).

sub_elements.name: Change Description for This Version
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.version.diff: 'Initial release of the LoRA fine-tune on the DeepLoc subcellular-localization training split using AdamW for 100 epochs.'
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear change description for the version, identifying this as the initial release with training configuration details.

sub_elements.name: Owners / Contributors Identified
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.contributors[0]: name: 'SaProtHub maintainers', role: developed_by, affiliation: 'Westlake Representation Learning Lab' (but no email, ORCID, or individual names); contributors[1]: name: 'Westlake-repl team (base SaProt)', role: contributed_by, affiliation: 'Westlake University'
sub_elements.quality_note: Contributors identified with roles and affiliations, but only team/group names provided (no individual names, emails, or ORCIDs per CRediT-style taxonomy).

sub_elements.name: Citation or BibTeX Provided
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.citations[0]: style: IEEE, citation for the model; citations[1]: style: IEEE, citation for SaProt paper 'J. Su et al., "SaProt: Protein Language Modeling with Structure-aware Vocabulary," in ICLR, 2024.'
sub_elements.quality_note: Two IEEE-style citations provided. While BibTeX would be preferable, the structured citations are machine-parseable and include sufficient information.

elements.name: Scientific Motivation and Funding Transparency
elements.description: Does the metadata state why the model exists and who funded it?

sub_elements.name: Motivation / Use Case Rationale
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.overview: 'This model classifies protein sequences into one of ten subcellular localization classes...'; considerations.use_cases: 4 use cases including annotation pipelines, proteomics filtering, benchmarking, and educational reference
sub_elements.quality_note: Clear motivation stated (subcellular localization classification) with multiple concrete use cases.

sub_elements.name: Primary Intended Use Articulated
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.use_cases[0]: 'Predicting subcellular localization for protein-annotation pipelines'; pipeline_tag: 'text-classification'
sub_elements.quality_note: Primary intended use clearly articulated as protein annotation with specific deployment context (pipelines).

sub_elements.name: Mission Relevance Stated (Extended)
sub_elements.evidence: No mission_relevance field present in the YAML file
sub_elements.quality_note: Missing extended template mission_relevance field. No explicit alignment with DOE/NIH/NSF research programs or domain mission stated.

sub_elements.name: Funding Source / Grant Agency Listed
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.contributors have affiliations ('Westlake Representation Learning Lab', 'Westlake University') but no funding sources, grant numbers, or agencies (NIH, NSF, DOE) mentioned
sub_elements.quality_note: No funding sources or grant agencies documented despite institutional affiliations being present.

sub_elements.name: Acknowledgement of Computing / Platform Support
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.description mentions 'single GPU' but no acknowledgement of computing facilities, cloud credits, or supporting institutions
sub_elements.quality_note: No acknowledgement of computing platform support, supercomputing facilities, or institutional computing resources.

elements.name: Training and Evaluation Transparency
elements.description: Can training and evaluation procedures be replicated or understood?

sub_elements.name: Training Procedure Documented
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.description: 'LoRA fine-tuning of SaProt_650M_AF2 on the DeepLoc subcellular-localization train split...'; reproducibility_info.hyperparameters with loss, optimizer, schedule, epochs, augmentation (LoRA), regularization (weight_decay)
sub_elements.quality_note: Comprehensive training procedure documented including loss function (cross-entropy), optimizer (AdamW), hyperparameters, epochs (100), and optimization techniques (LoRA).

sub_elements.name: Evaluation Procedure Documented
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics reports test accuracy but no model_parameters.training_procedure.evaluation_procedure or detailed evaluation protocol (metrics computation, slicing strategy beyond test set mention)
sub_elements.quality_note: Evaluation metrics reported but evaluation procedure (protocol, metrics computation details, evaluation frequency) not documented.

sub_elements.name: Reproducibility Information (Extended)
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters present but no separate ReproducibilityInfo with seeds, deterministic flags, environment pins, container hashes, or mission_relevance section
sub_elements.quality_note: Hyperparameters documented but missing extended reproducibility information (random seeds, deterministic flags, pinned environment, container specifications).

sub_elements.name: Open-Source Code Linked
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[2]: 'https://github.com/westlake-repl/SaProt'; model_details.documentation includes usage example with saprot_hub import
sub_elements.quality_note: GitHub repository link provided with code for the SaProt framework and usage examples.

sub_elements.name: External Standards or References Cited
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.citations[1]: SaProt ICLR 2024 paper; model_details.references[3]: UniProt subcellular location documentation; references[4]: DeepLoc 2.0 service
sub_elements.quality_note: External standards cited including academic paper (ICLR 2024), benchmark suite (DeepLoc 2.0), and domain-specific documentation (UniProt).

elements.name: Performance Evaluation and Limitations Disclosure
elements.description: Does the metadata communicate performance, known risks, biases, and limitations?

sub_elements.name: Quantitative Performance Metrics Reported
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[0]: type: 'test accuracy', value: 85.75, slice: 'DeepLoc subcellular localization test set (10-way)', unit: '%'
sub_elements.quality_note: Quantitative metric (test accuracy 85.75%) reported with slice (DeepLoc test set, 10-way classification) and unit.

sub_elements.name: Performance Across Sub-populations / Slices
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[0].slice: 'DeepLoc subcellular localization test set (10-way)' but no per-class, per-organism, or per-subgroup performance breakdown
sub_elements.quality_note: Only aggregate test accuracy reported. No per-class accuracy, per-organism performance, or other sub-population slicing provided.

sub_elements.name: Confidence Intervals or Error Bars
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[0] has value: 85.75 but no confidence_interval or value_error fields
sub_elements.quality_note: Single point estimate provided without confidence intervals, standard deviations, or error bars.

sub_elements.name: Limitations Section Present
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.limitations: 3 limitations including training data bias (SwissProt organism coverage), structural input requirement, and single-label constraint
sub_elements.quality_note: Explicit limitations documented with concrete failure modes: organism bias, structural dependency, and multi-label limitation.

sub_elements.name: Tradeoffs / Risks Acknowledged
sub_elements.evidence: considerations.tradeoffs[0]: '650M-parameter base provides higher accuracy than the 35M variant (85.75% vs ~80%) at higher inference cost. LoRA keeps trainable parameter count small...'; ethical_considerations addresses drug discovery risk
sub_elements.quality_note: Tradeoffs acknowledged (accuracy vs. computational cost, model size vs. inference time) and ethical risk (mis-localization in drug discovery) documented.

elements.name: Cross-Platform and Community Integration
elements.description: Does the model card connect to wider model ecosystems, benchmarks, and standards?

sub_elements.name: Published on a Recognized Platform
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.path: 'https://huggingface.co/SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M'; library_name: 'transformers'; model_details.references[0]: HuggingFace Hub URL
sub_elements.quality_note: Published on HuggingFace Hub, a recognized model hosting platform, with transformers library integration.

sub_elements.name: Cross-referenced DOIs or Related Model Links
sub_elements.evidence: model_details.references[1]: base model 'https://huggingface.co/westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'; base_model: 'westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2'; citations reference SaProt ICLR 2024 paper (though DOI not explicitly provided)
sub_elements.quality_note: Base model cross-referenced with HuggingFace Hub link and academic paper citation. No DOI explicitly provided, but paper citation includes venue (ICLR 2024).

sub_elements.name: Benchmark Results (Papers with Code style)
sub_elements.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics[0]: test accuracy 85.75% on DeepLoc test set; datasets[0]: 'SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'; tags include 'deeploc'
sub_elements.quality_note: Benchmark result reported on standard dataset (DeepLoc 2.0) with metric (accuracy 85.75%), suitable for leaderboard comparison.

sub_elements.name: Standards / Schema Conformance Stated
sub_elements.evidence: schema_version: '0.0.2' (line 9); comment at top references 'Schema: src/model_card_schema/schema/model_card_schema.yaml'
sub_elements.quality_note: Schema version explicitly stated (0.0.2) with reference to the Model Card schema definition.

sub_elements.name: Datasets Linked to Datasheets / D4D References
sub_elements.evidence: model_parameters.data[0].link: 'https://huggingface.co/datasets/SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'; datasets[0]: 'SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'; model_details.references[4]: DeepLoc 2.0 service link
sub_elements.quality_note: Dataset linked to HuggingFace Datasets page (SaProtHub mirror) and DeepLoc 2.0 service. While not a formal datasheet (D4D), the dataset is registered and accessible.

strengths: Exceptional model discovery metadata with complete identification (HF Hub ID, name, 8 domain-specific tags, multiple reference links)
Comprehensive ethical and responsible AI documentation including specific bias disclosures (species bias, class imbalance), out-of-scope uses, and mitigation strategies
Detailed architecture specification with LoRA configuration (rank 16, alpha 32, dropout 0) and layer-level detail (Q/K/V projections, FFN)
Strong reuse and interoperability support with MIT license, base model lineage (westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2), and comprehensive hyperparameters
Excellent cross-platform integration with HuggingFace Hub publication, benchmark results (DeepLoc 2.0, 85.75% accuracy), and dataset links
Clear limitations documentation addressing organism bias, structural input requirements, and single-label constraints
Detailed input/output specifications with structure-aware (SA) sequence format and 10-class interpretation
weaknesses: Missing compute infrastructure details (Element 5): No specific GPU model, compute hours, or energy consumption beyond 'single GPU' and FP16
Incomplete provenance documentation (Element 6): Contributors only have team names, no individual names, emails, or ORCIDs per CRediT taxonomy
Missing funding transparency (Element 7): No funding sources, grant agencies, or grant numbers documented despite institutional affiliations
No mission relevance field (Element 7): Extended template mission_relevance field absent (DOE/NIH/NSF alignment not stated)
Evaluation procedure not documented (Element 8): Metrics reported but evaluation protocol, metrics computation details, and evaluation frequency not specified
Missing extended reproducibility information (Element 8): No random seeds, deterministic flags, pinned environment, or container specifications
No per-class or sub-population performance (Element 9): Only aggregate test accuracy reported; no per-class, per-organism, or demographic slicing
No confidence intervals (Element 9): Single point estimate (85.75%) without error bars, standard deviations, or confidence intervals
Model file format not specified (Element 2): Serialization format (safetensors, PyTorch .bin, ONNX) not explicitly stated
No computing platform acknowledgements (Element 7): No mention of supercomputing facilities, cloud credits, or institutional computing support
recommendations: Add model_parameters.compute_infrastructure with hardware details (GPU model/count), total compute hours, and energy consumption if available
Expand model_details.contributors to include individual names, emails, and ORCIDs following CRediT taxonomy (e.g., Su, J. as first author with ORCID)
Add funding source documentation in contributors or mission_relevance section with grant agencies (NSF, NIH, DOE) and program names if applicable
Populate mission_relevance extended field if applicable (e.g., DOE BER Biological Systems Science Division alignment for proteomics)
Document model_parameters.training_procedure.evaluation_procedure with evaluation protocol, metrics computation details, and validation frequency

Add extended reproducibility fields: random seeds, deterministic flags (torch.use_deterministic_algorithms), pinned environment (conda env YAML or requirements.txt hash), container image hash if containerized
Expand quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics with per-class accuracy for all 10 subcellular localization classes and per-organism performance (human vs. E. coli vs. yeast) if data available
Add confidence intervals or error bars to performance metrics (e.g., from multiple runs or bootstrapping)
Explicitly state model file format in model_details.path or model_parameters notes (e.g., 'safetensors format' or 'PyTorch state_dict')
Add BibTeX citation format alongside IEEE style for better machine-readability (e.g., @inproceedings{su2024saprot, ...})
Consider adding acknowledgement of computing platform support if institutional resources were used (e.g., university HPC cluster, AWS credits)
